nodes.label: The neocortex final architecture (v2.0.0, frozen)
nodes.rationale: The whole two-brain chip in one file: cheap brain + namer + drift-gated economy + lifelong maintenance loop, tuned and locked at commit 59d2720; validated by Phase 10.

nodes.label: The spine (direction not magnitude / density != class)
nodes.rationale: Learning must preserve and read the class DIRECTION, never a magnitude (energy, rank, variance, confidence, distance). The recurring failure is density!=class; paid nine times across the phases.

nodes.label: Two-brain neocortex (80% SCFF + 20% namer)
nodes.rationale: A large unsupervised SCFF front (~80%) learns cheaply forward-only; a small precise namer (~20%) puts labels on what the front organized. The bet: direction is the one expensive thing in learning, so pay for it once where it counts.

nodes.label: The namer (SLDA deployed / RanPAC reference)
nodes.rationale: The 20% GD is a ROLE, not a method: the committed head has NO gradient — a closed-form streaming analytic head, because the cheap brain already did the representational work so naming reduces to a convex one-shot solve.

nodes.label: RanPAC (namer, accuracy/spine reference)
nodes.rationale: Frozen random ReLU projection + running-Gram ridge prototype W=(G+lambda I)^-1 M; gradient-free, closed-form, streaming; ties a tuned MLP, leads on natural digits, passes the A6 gate.

nodes.label: Deep SLDA (deployed namer)
nodes.rationale: Streaming per-class means + one shared tied covariance, classify by the LDA rule; deployed because it is 69x cheaper than RanPAC on the ADC meter and ties live. The tied covariance is the anisotropy fix (not a multimodal mixture).

nodes.label: Awake gate (DDM on error-EMA) — safety mechanism
nodes.rationale: Fires an expensive namer update only when a drift trigger says the cheap path stalled; meters the 80/20 real (GD-share 0.121). Crux inversion: firing MORE forgets MORE, so the gate is a continual-safety mechanism, not just thrift. Deployed on the error-EMA; a class-direction tap-drift trigger was validated but not shipped.

nodes.label: Lifelong maintenance loop (sleep + LUT, grid-4)
nodes.rationale: Periodic sleep re-forwards the bounded Hippocampus LUT through the current SCFF map, rebuilds the Gram, re-solves; all-tap consolidation, grid-4 cadence. Cheap because only the readout is replayed (the bulk doesn't forget).

nodes.label: Bulk rotates but does not forget
nodes.rationale: Measured with a re-fit probe (rotation factored out): the SCFF map rotates ~36% (a fixed head goes stale) but an optimal re-fit probe still decodes the old classes. So a periodic sleep re-solve suffices and a drift-slowdown (N2) is not mandatory.

nodes.label: CBRS bounded-LUT eviction (committed)
nodes.rationale: Class-balanced reservoir keeps prototypes balanced so the buffer spans the class DIRECTIONS at a tight budget; herding (keep the mean, a magnitude) ties it as the buffer-spine null; cap grows with #classes (a scaling law).

nodes.label: Proto-reanchor read-side defense
nodes.rationale: Re-forward the raw LUT through the current bulk under the same device offset -> drift-free, shift-consistent prototypes; recovers retention 0.79->0.99. The plan's own sleep mechanism applied under shift, direction-grounded, no new organ — discharges the Phase-6 read-side debt.

nodes.label: The substrate (Scap, crossbar, mono-forward)
nodes.rationale: Scap = charge (magnitude) + SRAM bit (sign); crossbar computes in the wires; mono-forward dual-rail carries two activation worlds through one shared crossbar. A forward-only local rule removes the transposed crossbar + stored activations + cross-layer chain — the analog-expensive parts. ADCs dominate the metered energy.

hyperedges.label: Two-brain neocortex loop (SCFF bulk + namer + gated economy + maintenance)
The spine paid across the design (direction, not magnitude)
